rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations in the component type scope
Place implementation-level bindings such as assignments in the component implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
The authoritative source for component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections is the input AADL model
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Ensure that assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports are placed in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports in implementation scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
